creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
I can't reproduce. Check wayfire.ini syntax and XKB syntax.

  # Query available XKB layouts in xkeyboard-config via libxkbcommon
  $ xkbcli list | sed -n "/- \(layout\): \(be\)/ { s//xkb_\1 = \2/; N; s/\n.*: \(.*\)/(\1)/; s/('')//; p; }"
  xkb_layout = be
  xkb_layout = be(oss)
  xkb_layout = be(oss_latin9)
  xkb_layout = be(iso-alternate)
  xkb_layout = be(nodeadkeys)
  xkb_layout = be(wang)

  # Create a new Wayfire config with standard Belgian layout (wf-shell is autostarted by default)
  $ printf '[input]\nxkb_layout = be\n' >~/.config/wayfire.ini

Alternatively, configure XKB layout in Wayfire via GUI tool.

  $ pkg install wcm
  $ wcm
  <Click on "Input">
  <Enter "be" in "XKB layout" field>
